person.name: Gerard Van Der Aa Van Boecsteyl

nameForms.fullText: Gerard Van Der Aa Van Boecsteyl

km.Voor 30-7-1314: ថ្ងៃទី 30 ខែកក្កដា ឆ្នាំ 1314

zh.lifeSummary: Gerard Van Der Aa Van Boecsteyl於大約1258年出生。他和N Willemsdr. Van Ghestel至少有1個女兒。他去世於1314年7月30日享年57歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Gerard Van Der Aa Van Boecsteyl于大约1258年出生。他和N Willemsdr. Van Ghestel至少有1个女儿。他去世于1314年7月30日享年57岁。

nameMeanings.name: Van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Der
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Hungarian Zoltan, Istvan, Janos, Lajos.
Hungarian (Dér): from the old personal name Dér of Turkic origin, or a nickname for a gray-haired man, from the vocabulary word dér ‘hoarfrost’.
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 謝, see   Xie   1.

variants.name: Xie
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Hong, Li, Ming, Ying, Min, Qing, Yuan, Bin, Hui, Ping, Wei, Xiaoli, Ding, Chang, Lan, Quan, Cong, Dang, Hai, Tian, Yiping.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 謝, meaning ‘thank’ in Chinese: (i) from Xie (謝), the name of an ancient state (possibly located in Nanyang in Henan province), annexed around the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ).Later this place was probably granted to Shen Bo (the Earl of Shen), uncle of King Xuan of Zhou (reigned 827–782  BC ). After it was annexed during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), the name of the state was adopted as a surname. (ii) adopted in place of the surname Zhi-Le (直勒) by Xie Xiaozheng (謝孝政), an official during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Aa
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Long, Minh, Muoi, Phan, Phat, Vinh, Cuong, Hien, Hoa, Lam, Lan.  Chinese Wing, Chi, Kin, Cheuk, Chi Wai, Chuen, Hon, Keung, Wah, Wai, Chiu, Ho.
Chinese: alternative Mandarin form of the surname 敖, see   Ao  .
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 歐 and 區, see   Ou   1 and 2.

variants.name: Auerbach
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Hyman, Moshe, Emanuel, Isidor, Bronia, Chaim, Faigy, Menachem, Meyer, Yechezkel.
German: habitational name from any of several places in southern Germany and Austria called Auerbach ‘the stream at the water meadow’. See also 2,   Au  , and   Bach  .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name from the town of Auerbach in the Upper Palatinate, Bavaria. Compare   Averbach  .

variants.name: Ao
meanings: Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 敖, meaning both ‘rambling’ and ‘deposed king without posthumous titles’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the personal name of Tai Ao (太敖), teacher of the legendary king Zhuanxu (traditional dates: 2514–2437 BC ). (ii) it is said that the surname is also borne by descendants of deposed kings of the former state of Chu, with no posthumous titles, during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ).

variants.name: Auer
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Alois, Hans, Erwin, Matthias, Siegfried, Bernd, Bernhard, Erna, Franz, Fritz, Hermann, Horst.

German (mainly Bavaria): topographic name for someone living by a water meadow, German Au, or a habitational name for someone from a place called Au or Aue (see Au and Aue ). This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Czechia, and Slovenia.

(ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Gerard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Old French name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, introduced to Britain by the Normans. It is derived from gār, gēr ‘spear’ + hard ‘brave, hardy, strong’. In the later Middle Ages this was a more common name than Gerald , with which it was sometimes confused. Nowadays it is less common, surviving mainly among Roman Catholics, in honour of the many saints of this name.

variants.name: Gerrard
meanings: Variant spelling of   Gerard  , in part from the surname derived from the given name in the Middle Ages.

variants.name: Jerrard
meanings: Rare variant spelling of   Gerard  , probably influenced by the form of the modern surname, if not a transferred use.

en.lifeSummary: Gerard Van Der Aa Van Boecsteyl was born about 1258. He had at least 1 daughter with N Willemsdr. Van Ghestel. He died on 30 July 1314, at the age of 57.

fr.lifeSummary: Gerard Van Der Aa Van Boecsteyl est né environ 1258. Il a eu au moins 1 fille avec N Willemsdr. Van Ghestel. Il est décédé le 30 juillet 1314, à l’âge de 57 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Gerard Van Der Aa Van Boecsteyl wurde um 1258 geboren. Er hatte mit N Willemsdr. Van Ghestel mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb am 30. Juli 1314 im Alter von 57 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Gerard Van Der Aa Van Boecsteyl è nato il circa 1258. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlia con N Willemsdr. Van Ghestel. Morì il 30 luglio 1314, all'età di 57 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Gerard Van Der Aa Van Boecsteylは約1258年に生まれた。彼はN Willemsdr. Van Ghestelとの間に少なくとも1人の娘をもうけた。彼は1314年7月30日に57歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Gerard Van Der Aa Van Boecsteyl(은)는 대략 1258년 태어났다. 그는 N Willemsdr. Van Ghestel(와)과 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1314년 7월 30일 57세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Gerard Van Der Aa Van Boecsteyl nasceu aproximadamente 1258. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filha com N Willemsdr. Van Ghestel. Ele faleceu em 30 de julho de 1314, com 57 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Gerard Van Der Aa Van Boecsteyl родился около 1258 г. У него была по крайней мере 1 дочь от N Willemsdr. Van Ghestel. Он умер 30 июля 1314 г. в возрасте 57 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Gerard Van Der Aa Van Boecsteyl nació aproximadamente 1258. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hija con N Willemsdr. Van Ghestel. Murió el 30 de julio de 1314, a la edad de 57 años.
